meaning: n.重新扣留(拘捕)v.重新拘捕(逮捕)
examples: Their likelihood of rearrest only drops back to average levels after six years, the number-crunchers found.
In 2009 it said that impunity "continued for most perpetrators, exacerbated by police threatening victims with rearrest or the arrest of relatives if they lodged complaints".
Even more shocking is the fact that the number and rate of imprisonment have more than doubled over the past twenty years, and recidivism — that is the rate for rearrest — is more than 60 percent.
